subject: Medium armor
method: full human/LLM read of transcript 038 (not the heuristic)
note: All 33 tiers below were confirmed to match the stored tierlists.json exactly. The 'why' field is new content extracted from the transcript. Medium armor caps Dex bonus at +2 unless stated ('uncapped' armors are prized); rated by power when acquired. 'Dark Justiciar Half Plate'=DJ half plate; '(Rare)' version is the upgraded one.

Githyanki Half Plate.why: 15 base AC and Lae'zel's starting armor, so with +2 Dex and a shield you hit 19 AC from level 1 — near-unhittable early, and it stays best-in-slot for medium/heavy users through a big chunk of Act 1.

+1 Breastplate.why: 15 AC and 1 piercing resistance (the most relevant physical type, vs early goblin archers); one is stealable in the Druid Grove, giving you a crucial second high-AC set early — a top early-game priority for most parties.

Luminous Armour.why: 15 AC plus Radiating Shockwave: dealing radiant damage stacks Radiating Orb (up to -10 to enemy attack rolls) in a 10ft radius — arguably a top-3 item in the game, instantly winning boss fights, and strong even with only incidental radiant damage.

Adamantine Scale Mail.why: 16 AC, -1 to all incoming damage, critical-hit immunity, and Reeling on attackers who hit you — a cohesive defensive package that removes the random crit spikes that lose fights; best-in-slot tank armor for most of the game.

Yuan-Ti Scale Mail.why: 15 AC that uses your FULL Dex bonus (uncapped) plus +1 initiative and no stealth disadvantage — the highest-AC setup in the game for Dex characters and the gold-standard Act 2 armor for them.

(Rare) Dark Justiciar Half-Plate.why: The very rare DJ plate: 17 AC (highest non-uncapped in Act 2), advantage on Con saves, advantage on stealth while obscured, Shield of Faith once/rest, plus -2 damage and necrotic reflection while it's active — strictly better than the base version.

Elven Chain.why: 14 AC, +2 initiative, advantage on Dex saves, and grants proficiency — implemented as 'counts as cloth', so non-proficient casters/Rogues wear it freely AND it stacks with Bracers of Defense, monk speed, etc.; a superb high-AC + initiative option for those classes.

Armour of Agility.why: 17 base AC, fully uncapped Dex, and +2 saves — the highest AC values in the game (30+ is easy), making high-Dex characters near-invincible to physical attacks, while the +2 saves cover the fear/dominate effects that are their only real weakness.

Flame Enamelled Armour.why: 16 AC, +2 initiative, +2 saves, fire (and on-cast cold) resistance, and Fire Shield once/rest — a fantastic all-rounder that lets a high-AC character also go first; the safe default if no other S-tier armor calls to you.

Scale Mail.why: 14 AC non-magical, the Fighter's starting armor that also drops off the nautiloid devils — a solid +1 AC upgrade over Shadowheart's chain shirt for the very early game, quickly superseded.

+1 Half Plate.why: 16 AC with 1 piercing resistance, buyable from level 6 — likely your first 16 AC medium armor and a meaningful party-wide upgrade, though less critical than others since heavy-armor users start converting around now.

+2 Hide.why: 14 AC, +1 to Dex saves/checks, and +1 initiative from level 5 — likely your first medium-armor initiative source, and initiative is good enough in BG3 that it's worth the 1 AC drop versus Githyanki Half Plate.

+2 Scale Mail.why: 16 AC, 1 slashing resistance, and +1 initiative — high AC plus an initiative bonus makes it a strict upgrade over the +1 Half Plate or +2 Hide for many characters, though defensive/Dex builds prefer Adamantine/Yuan-Ti.

Dark Justiciar Half Plate.why: 16 AC, advantage on Con saves, advantage on stealth while obscured, and Shield of Faith once/rest — a great spellcaster package (high AC + concentration help), just edged out by Adamantine Scale Mail and Luminous Armour for the classes that want it.

+1 Chain Shirt.why: 14 AC and 1 slashing resistance from the first merchant; only useful for a third medium-armor user since you'll already have Githyanki Half Plate and a stealable +1 Breastplate — a small, quickly-outgrown upgrade.

+2 Chain Shirt.why: 15 AC, 1 slashing resistance, and +1 to Dex saves/checks (level 5); marginally useful for a stealthy medium-armor character making actual stealth checks, but a minor upgrade not worth seeking out.

Sharpened Snare Cuirass.why: 14 AC but uncapped Dex (so high AC for high-Dex builds) plus disadvantage on enemy saves vs your Ensnaring effects — enables a fun entangling build with Cruel/Woad Shield/gloves, though Yuan-Ti Scale Mail's higher AC keeps it in B.

+2 Half Plate.why: 17 AC with 2 piercing resistance (level 9); possibly your first 17 AC armor, but by then high-Dex/caster/defensive characters are committed to better sets — a fine second armor for an unspecialized character.

Unwanted Masterwork Scalemail.why: 16 AC, uncapped Dex, and fire resistance — a powerful uncapped armor, but Yuan-Ti Scale Mail (+1 initiative) and Armour of Agility (+2 saves, 17 AC) are available around the same time and better, leaving it without a niche.

Chain Shirt.why: 13 AC non-magical (Shadowheart's start); normally ignorable, but a Ranger (11 AC leather) or low-Con Barbarian can use an early-dropping chain shirt to bump their AC.

Dark Justiciar Mail.why: 13 AC, no stealth disadvantage, and 1d4 caustic retaliation while obscured; too low AC to wear normally, but theoretically usable in an armor-of-agathys/fire-shield retaliation build for the extra necrotic-style damage.

Hedge Wanderer Armour.why: 13 AC with +1 Nature (proficiency-coded, so +2 with expertise), +1 Dex saves/checks, and no stealth disadvantage; the AC drop isn't worth it full-time, but you can swap it in to pass a specific Nature or Sleight of Hand check.

+1 Scale Mail.why: 15 AC with 1 slashing resistance (levels 4-6); by the time it appears you have better 15 AC options (Githyanki, +1 Breastplate, maybe Luminous), so it's only a tiny upgrade worth chasing for hardcore optimizers.

The Jolty Vest.why: A +1 Chain Shirt (14 AC, slashing resistance) that, while you hold lightning charges, makes attackers save (DC14) or be Shocked; the DC is easy to pass and reactive Shocked is a weak effect, so it's a marginal Lightning-Charge pick.

Barkskin Armour.why: Locks your AC at exactly 16 (no Dex or shield bonuses) with advantage on Con saves; you sacrifice a lot of AC for those Con saves, which is counterproductive for concentration — only for a forced-low-Dex challenge build.

Psionic Ward Armour.why: 15 AC, and for gith (or anyone using Disguise Self) psychic resistance plus 1d4 heal on a successful spell save; psychic resistance is genuinely rare, making it a niche swap-in for psychic-heavy fights only.

Damaged Scale Mail.why: 12 base AC non-magical; by the time it drops in Act 1 you have far better armor and it barely sells — no reason to ever pick it up.

Breastplate.why: 14 AC, identical to Scale Mail but with no stealth disadvantage (which barely matters) and only available near the end of Act 1, when 14 AC is worthless — never worth picking up.

Half Plate.why: Functionally identical to Githyanki Half Plate (15 AC) but only sold near the end of Act 1, where 15 AC non-magical armor is no longer valuable — never worth buying.

+1 Hide Armour.why: 13 AC, no stealth disadvantage, +1 Dex saves/checks — a strictly worse Hedge Wanderer Armour (no Nature bonus), available at the same time and place, so there's no reason to ever buy it.

Slippery Chain Shirt.why: 13 AC and a free disengage when you heal, but medium-armor characters rarely want to leave melee and the low AC isn't worth it; even the hoped-for Cloak of Cunning Brume combo doesn't trigger — a cool idea that's too weak.

The Oak Father's Embrace.why: 13 AC with 1d6 radiant retaliation against undead (but you take extra radiant from beasts) — only a marginal swap-in over the Dark Justiciar Mail for undead retaliation fights; otherwise just a 13 AC armor with no real ability.

+2 Breastplate.why: 16 AC, 1 piercing resistance, +1 Dex saves/checks (level 9); by Act 3 it's outclassed — the +2 Scale Mail gives the same AC plus +2 initiative, so there's no reason to use this.
